meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to build a SaaS starter kit and overcome the no portfolio trap. An intermediate-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for speed.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing clients with empty hands. By building a production-ready SaaS starter kit, you create a reusable codebase that serves as a living portfolio. This approach proves your engineering prowess while drastically reducing your future development time, allowing you to ship high-quality client projects in days, not weeks.
content.intro:
The blank page is the freelancer's greatest enemy. You have the skills, but without a track record, clients see only risk. You are stuck in the 'no portfolio' purgatory, waiting for a project to prove your worth, while the projects themselves require proof you already exist. It is a cycle that kills momentum before it ever begins.

But what if you didn't wait for a client to build your portfolio? By architecting a modular, high-performance SaaS starter kit, you transform your workflow into a product. This isn't just code; it's a competitive advantage that allows you to demonstrate sophisticated infrastructure, authentication flows, and payment integrations—all before you even sign your first contract.

steps.title: Define the Architecture Strategy
steps.description: Establish the core stack (Next.js, Supabase, Tailwind) and project structure to ensure modularity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-grade architecture diagram and folder structure for a multi-tenant SaaS starter kit using Next.js 14, App Router, and Supabase.

steps.title: Build the Authentication Layer
steps.description: Implement robust, secure authentication that handles role-based access control (RBAC) out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure auth service component with Supabase, including middleware for protected routes and automatic session handling.

steps.title: Integrate Billing and Subscriptions
steps.description: Connect Stripe to your starter kit to demonstrate mastery of complex SaaS revenue models.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Stripe integration with webhooks for subscription management, including a pricing table component.

steps.title: Develop the Dashboard UI
steps.description: Design a clean, professional dashboard shell that you can easily reskin for any client project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a responsive dashboard layout component with a collapsible sidebar and protected layout wrapper.

steps.title: Implement Testing and CI/CD
steps.description: Add automated tests to prove your code quality to prospective clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a Playwright testing suite for core user flows including auth, billing, and dashboard navigation.

pro_tips: Always include a 'demo mode' toggle in your starter kit so clients can click through the UI without needing to sign up.
Document your code as if it were an open-source library; clients value documentation as much as the app itself.
Treat your starter kit as a living repo; every time you build a feature for a client, refactor it into your base kit.
Use environment variable abstraction to ensure you can swap databases or APIs in under 10 minutes.
Include a 'Performance Benchmark' report in your README to show clients that your code is optimized for SEO and speed.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is polished, deploy it to Vercel and create a 'Portfolio Case Study' page that explains the technical decisions you made during the build process.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting the 'no portfolio' excuse hold your career back. By building your own SaaS starter kit, you become a developer who ships. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
